meta.philosophy: Describe your project's architectural philosophy here
meta.stack: List your tech stack (e.g., React, Node.js, PostgreSQL)

guardrails.name: No Direct Database Access from UI
guardrails.description: UI components must not directly access the database. All data access must go through API endpoints or service layers.

guardrails.name: API Versioning Required
guardrails.description: All API endpoints must include version prefix (e.g., /api/v1/). Breaking changes require new version.

guardrails.name: Proper Error Handling
guardrails.description: All async operations must have try-catch blocks. Errors must be logged and user-friendly messages returned.

guardrails.name: Authentication on Protected Routes
guardrails.description: All non-public API endpoints must require authentication. Use middleware to verify tokens.

guardrails.name: Input Validation Required
guardrails.description: All user inputs must be validated before processing. Use validation libraries and sanitize data.

guardrails.name: No Hardcoded Secrets
guardrails.description: API keys, passwords, and secrets must never be hardcoded. Use environment variables.

guardrails.name: Consistent Naming Conventions
guardrails.description: Follow project naming conventions: camelCase for variables/functions, PascalCase for classes/components.

guardrails.name: Single Responsibility Principle
guardrails.description: Functions and classes should have a single, well-defined purpose. Avoid god objects.
